rulings.Case Title: MARGERY JAQUITH HOLLISTER VS. GREGORY OVALLE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner?S Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 16, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER MARGERY HOLLISTER Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment is continued to August 26, 2021. By no later than August 13, 2021 Respondent shall file a supplemental opposition, if any. By no later than August 19, 2021 petitioner may file a supplemental reply. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO HUERTA-OLIVIER ET AL VS. 320 ALEMANY LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 16, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT VIKING SERVICES CORPORATION, QUANG SAU MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. Plaintiffs do not dispute that the moving defendants are property managers and agents of the owner/landlord. By its express terms, Civil Code 1942.4 imposes duties on and provides for a claim against a landlord. Plaintiff fails to cite, nor is the court aware of, any authority that a property manager may be held liable under section. 1942.4. Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal. App. 3d 903, 930 is inapposite because that decision does not discuss section 1942.4 =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARIA ELISA MATUS ET AL VS. FREEDOM WEST CO-OPERATIVE HOUSING PROJECT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motions And Motions To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 16, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT FREEDOM WEST HOMES CORPORATION Notice Of Motions And Motions To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is GRANTED. Assuming without deciding that the court has discretion to deny this motion per CCP 1281.2(c), the court declines to exercise that discretion because MacFarlane Partners, LLC has not appeared in this case notwithstanding that the case was filed 9 months ago. Plaintiffs have not shown that Freedom West waived its right to arbitrate the claims alleged against it. Nor have plaintiffs shown that the arbitration provision in the bylaws is unenforceable. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
